description: High-performance, workflow-driven Shop Management System for automotive repair shops. Manages work orders, jobs, line items, appointments, with real-time notifications, approvals, and proactive sales recovery.

folders.name: config
folders.aiNotes: Do not hardcode credentials; use .env variables. Config is only for connections/settings.

folders.name: controllers
folders.aiNotes: Controllers contain business logic only; never modify models directly inside controllers.

folders.name: middleware
folders.aiNotes: Middleware is for request processing only, no business logic.

folders.name: models
folders.aiNotes: Models define the database schema only; do not put logic or validation beyond schema constraints.

folders.name: public
folders.aiNotes: Only static files like stylesheets, scripts, and images. No server logic here.

folders.name: routes
folders.aiNotes: Routes map HTTP endpoints to controllers; no business logic here.

folders.name: services
folders.role: Reusable helpers, API calls, WebSocket helpers
folders.aiNotes: Use services for integrations, repeated logic, and WebSocket handling.

folders.name: views
folders.aiNotes: Render templates here; do not include API logic.

folders.name: node_modules
folders.aiNotes: Do not edit; auto-generated by npm install.

files.name: .env
files.aiNotes: Never commit live secrets. Use this for DB strings, JWT secrets, etc.

files.name: server.js
files.role: Application entry point; starts Express server and Socket.io
files.aiNotes: Contains startup logic only. Load middleware, routes, and services here.

files.name: package.json
files.aiNotes: Use scripts to run, build, or test the app.

files.name: package-lock.json
files.aiNotes: Do not edit manually; ensures reproducible builds.

files.name: README.md
files.aiNotes: Includes project overview, folder structure, tech stack, setup instructions, and AI guidance.

files.name: setup.completed.js
files.aiNotes: May contain initialization logic; do not rerun unless instructed.

architecture: Routes map endpoints to controllers only
Middleware handles auth, validation, and error handling only
Views are server-rendered templates; no SPA frameworks
Do not introduce new frameworks without approval
Follow naming conventions in existing files
aiGuidelines: Use README + folder structure as source of truth
Prioritize data integrity in code generation
Read existing files before suggesting changes
Follow naming conventions and coding patterns
Do not modify core files without approval
